dct_title_s: [Series of maps of Palestine from Fulton's Palestine]
dct_description_sm: [John Fulton] ; drawn by H. Kiepert ; engraved & printed by Wagner & Debes.
Coordinates approximate and based on Greenwich meridian.
7 bar scales on map: "Paces", "Yards", "Mètres", "English miles", "Geographical miles", "English stat. miles", and "Miles".
Shows topography, drainage, territorial boundaries of the tribes of Israel, travel routes, places of historical interest.
4 inset maps: Southern suburb -- Sinai enlarged -- Red Sea crossing -- The kingdoms of Israel and Judah.
Appears in Fulton's Palestine: the Holy Land as it was and as it is. Philadelphia, H.T. Coates & Co., 1900.

[1] General plan of Jerusalem -- [2] Environs of Jerusalem -- [3] Countries of the Bible showing also the lands of the captivities and the probable route of Abraham -- [4] Egypt and the Sinai Peninsula with the journeys of the Israelites to the promised land -- [5] The Holy Land as divided amongst the twelve tribes -- [6] The dominions of David & Solomon -- [7] The Holy Land in the time of Christ -- [8] The shores of the Sea of Gallilee on a larger scale -- [9] St. Paul's journeys -- [10] The environs of Jerusalem -- [11] Jerusalem from the Bible.
